description_long: This is a rounded, ancient-style ceramic vase with a single thick handle and a narrow, slightly flared spout. The body is adorned with hand-painted motifs, including stylized birds and floral patterns in earthy tones of black, brown, and ochre. The neck features a decorative band with repeated teardrop shapes and dots, adding to its ornate appearance. The surface shows a matte finish with visible brushwork and some patina, suggesting age or intentional antiquing. The handle is robust and curves from the rim to the shoulder of the vase, providing both functional and aesthetic value. The overall form is bulbous, tapering gently to a small, stable base.
description: A rounded, hand-painted ceramic vase with a single thick handle, decorated with bird and floral motifs in earthy tones.
description_view_0: Front view showing the spout, handle, and prominent bird and floral motifs on the body.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the curvature of the handle and the continuation of the painted floral patterns.
description_view_2: Rear angled view displaying the back of the handle and more subtle floral decorations.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a clear look at the second bird motif and the full handle arch.
